wants.description: Define user journey for external professionals with scoped read/write access

knows.value: External professional (psychologist, teacher, doctor, magistrate, family assistant) invited to access specific child dossier or section. Key steps: (1) Receive invitation email with scoped access link, (2) Authenticate (MFA required), (3) View assigned child dossier sections (scoped by role — magistrate sees judicial docs, doctor sees health data, teacher sees schooling), (4) Upload documents or reports within permitted scope, (5) Read/write in messaging thread if granted, (6) No access to placement request workflow or available places dashboard.
Platform is professionals-only. Children and families have no access. GDPR right of access requests handled offline.

wants.description: Define WCAG compliance level and specific accessibility constraints

knows.value: Target: WCAG 2.1 Level AA. Requirements: (1) Keyboard navigation for all interactions (placement request form, document upload, filters), (2) Screen reader support — semantic HTML, ARIA labels for dynamic content (notifications, real-time dashboard updates), (3) Color contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 for text, ≥3:1 for UI components, (4) Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements, (5) Form validation messages announced to screen readers, (6) PDF documents must be accessible (tagged PDFs), (7) Time-based notifications must not auto-dismiss (users can dismiss manually), (8) Alternative text for all document icons and status indicators.
Main adoption risk: departments themselves (internal resistance to change). Secondary: establishments and family assistants (digital literacy concerns). No onboarding/training plan yet defined.
External professionals (psychologists, doctors, teachers, magistrates, family assistants) need scoped read/write access. Closed by default, opened granularly via role/group management.

wants.description: Define mobile vs desktop priorities and breakpoint rationale

knows.value: Primary platform: Desktop (social workers spend most time at desk), but mobile is critical for establishments and external professionals (psychologists, family assistants on the move). Responsive strategy: desktop-first design, mobile-optimized for key flows (view placement requests, file reports, upload documents, check notifications). Breakpoints: mobile (<768px), tablet (768–1024px), desktop (≥1024px). Mobile: simplified dashboard, single-column layout, bottom tab navigation, touch-optimized inputs (date pickers, file upload). Desktop: multi-column dashboard, sidebar navigation, richer data tables with sorting/filtering.
Main adoption risk: departments themselves (internal resistance to change). Secondary: establishments and family assistants (digital literacy concerns). No onboarding/training plan yet defined.
